reviews.review_description: Search on YouTube for: Meta Quest Lab Italia; I have posted a detailed explanation of the mechanics. It should probably be 4 stars because it's a bit unpolished and still in Early Access, but the core idea is great and the price is incredibly low. It can only get better from here. Better to grab it now at this price.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback and your support! And yes, we're still working on polishing the game and adding new missions and improvements for the full launch!

reviews.review_description:
I’ve only completed the first level + the 2 tutorial lvls but man-o-man, I’m loving it so far. Feels like a combo of We Are One, Unloop, & Last Clockwinder.  Question for the devs… how many lvls are there?  I really don’t want this game to end, and hoping it’s not too short.  
From what I’ve played so far, it doesn’t feel Early Access to me… I would’ve assumed it was the final product if not told it was EA.  So obviously I haven’t experienced any glitches or bugs (maybe 1 or 2, but nothing game breaking). 

Couple requests: 
- please add the ability to skip dialogue. I’m prolly in the minority but I really don’t care about narrative in most games, especially puzzlers. I just wanna move onto the next lvl as soon as I finish the previous. 

- please add sitting options. I set my height to where I’m sitting but it doesn’t work. I have to be standing in order for my correct height to be measured.  Didn’t notice a Seated option but maybe I didn’t look hard enough.  

Other than those 2 small complaints, this game is a banger.  Get it before the price jumps (at least I’m assuming it will jump after EA).


UPDATE: Thanks for the reply! Though I wasn’t asking for subtitles to be added. I was asking for a SKIP button for all dialogue and cutscenes. Is that possible?

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing the game and taking the time to leave the positive feedback ! Currently there are 5 main missions and a few additional mission we call 'challenges' as they are in a 'training' environment and focused on improving some specific skills of your agent team! We also plan to add a few more missions by the end of the EA, so the experience feels even more substantial for the full launch.
Regarding your requests : subtitles, we'll take a look and see what we can do in a future update! For the seated option, it's already there, you have to go to the menu>setting>Play Area>Seated Play :) 
Hope you'll enjoy the rest of the game!

reviews.review_description:
Nice puzzle game.Playing this just after « unloop » (which is great imo) I felt home.

Nice concept, well executed, 
Atpmosphere is top notch.
And….feels refreshing.

I would just like to be able to crouch whenever I want and not automatically and be able to jump maybe ?…but this one could be exagerated 

But other than that the game is enjoyable as it is

Hopefully some new levels will be  added in the future 🤞

This has been one of my most anticipated games this year. I'm quite new to VR still so playing catch up.
I like to pace myself with games and not gobble them all up at once. So I'm only about halfway through, doing just a couple of missions.
This is a lot of fun though so far! Great ideas and setup! It reminds me of We Are One in the best ways!
The formula of commanding different agents yourself to work alongside each other is genius. The levels are only a few minutes, but the time taken is in the planning of it perfectly. Uncovering all the different things you can do and should do, coordinating, pulling off teamwork as one person, it's all really satisfying.
High-fiving the other character, swapping items, opening ways, different abilities, all work so well! This is what VR should be about!
Lots of care has been put in to the artwork and the content. Simple but also highly detailed.
The mix of characters is great, and the voice acting! The humour makes me chuckle and the thoughtfulness of it all is awesome! It's mostly easy to use, with just a couple of things not fully explained. But the joy does come in the trial and error of it all.

I'm not on Discord, but just to mention the couple of glitches I've come across. They aren't major, but just as a heads up...
On rewinding and retrying the train mission sometimes the robot would spawn in the train floor. I would have to rewind and such a few times to fix it.
Also, on the truth serum party mission, on one run through the disk was out of the drive when I got there and I couldn't pick it up. I had to restart the mission.
But these are minor issues. And to be expected. Other than that it all seems to run smoothly and is a real joy to play!
Oh yeah, I recommended this game to my friend for his son to try who has a Quest! He seems to be enjoying it so far!
And i also purchased your other game. I don't have much money but want to try to support as many developers as I can. And you guys seem to deserve support. Keep doing what you do! I look forward to more content! Thank you!

Spymaster in Early Access on Meta Quest 3 genuinely surprised me.

Even with some Early Access rough edges, I had an absolute blast with it. It’s clever, charming, satisfying, and one of those VR games that kept making me coming back for more. 

What impressed me most is how strongly it feels built for VR. The best moments are not just fun because they’re happening in a headset — they feel like they only really work because of VR.

It has minor glitches. It needs a bit more polish. And I definitely want more content. But the foundation here is really, really strong, and I came away genuinely excited about where this game could go.

This is one of the easiest Early Access recommendations I’ve made in a while.

You can watch my full review/impressions video on my YouTube channel: GameListPlus.

developer_response.body: Thanks for so much your impressions of the early access! Appreciate how thoughtful you are about the game and why things work that way! Already working on some of your feedback for our next update ;)

reviews.review_description: My Full Review is on Youtube - Lordsi VR - but The gameplay and Mechanics are Fun! The Story is a Little Lacking and short but Looking forward to the More Missions Soon!
Very fun and original, very quick to get you into the game. Good sense of narrative cadence/pacing

A great blend of stealth, strategy, and puzzle-solving. Every mission encourages you to think before you act, using gadgets, observation, and clever tactics rather than just rushing in. The unique game rewind VR mechanics make every infiltration and escape feel rewarding, while the varied missions keep the gameplay fresh and engaging. The attention to detail and satisfying progression make it difficult to put down.

This game is early release not quite fully polished but I just know its going to get here, well done devs.

reviews.review_title: Incredible art style and unique mechanic
reviews.review_description: The art is right up there with walkabout minigolf, it's sooo much better in the headset, there's little details on the characters that hover over their bodies and it works really well. The game loop is great, you have a set amount of time on the mission and you switch between 2 agents and use their unique skills - and you use your watch to rewind or fast forward in an overview map of the environment. I think it you die you shouldn't have to wait so long to rewind I'd like to grab the watch as I'm dying kinda thing. The voiced characters are great, I'm still pretty early on in the game but I'm very happy with this purchase and will update when I've finished all the missions

developer_response.body: Thanks for the support, hope you'll like the rest of the game!

reviews.review_title: ONE OF THOSE TOP NOTCH PUZZLE GAMES
reviews.review_description:
Spymaster does something very unique in VR, such an original concept, work through a puzzle, and rewind to see where you messed it up.
You really get hooked into this game, o don’t even mind the graphics are not the greatest on Quest 3
Tbe gameplay is where it matters, my head was spinning at times trying to work it all out, but take your time rewind and it all falls into place.
VR really needed something different and addictive and this is the game that has done it for me.
Highly recommended for puzzle fans.

I love stealth games and love the concept of puppeteering multiple characters asynchronously to solve a puzzle. This game combines both and it features a timeline control that is really cool. You can record parts of the timeline and re-record as much as you like if you aren't happy with the outcome. 

I haven't finished the game but spent some time with a couple of the levels, completing the bonus goals or just replaying for fun. So the game gives replayability and the levels have a decent level of complexity.

Now there's quite a bit of jank and the controls are a bit hit and miss: eg the climbing mechanic is great but you don't have a jump button and crouching is automatic (?). There are some bugs here and there as well (eg laser pointer UI not working on some buttons at times) and some plain wrong little design decisions that could be reverted (eg the camera moves when loading the screens, which will make some people sick and others may feel they lose balance). In my view these are little details and they don't undermine the value of the game. It is a really cool game mechanic and I can see myself playing the game over and over for a long time.
Another great VR work by Innerspace, i love all their games! This one is super fun to play, even still in Early Access!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for you support and glad Spymaster didn't disappoint ! :)

reviews.review_description: Highly recommend it. Love the InnerspaceVR team’s work and talent!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing the game, and we appreciate the kind words! ;)
